title: Resurrection
wikitext:
[[File:Shenron Revives Krillin.gif|right|thumb|350px|[[Shenron]] and [[Porunga]] are frequently asked to use their wish-granting abilities to resurrect those who have been killed]]

==Summary==
'''Resurrection''' is the ability to revive oneself or others after they have died. Often, characters can do one or the other, not both, however this is not always the case. The limits of this ability vary from fiction to fiction and from character to character; some may be only able to resurrect those who have recently died, while others may be capable of bringing those who have been dead for quite some time back to life, even if nothing remains.

When in the hands of a character who uses resurrection on themselves, giving them some degree of [[Immortality]], this ability often co-exists with [[Regeneration]], though the process can take some time. This ability is not to be confused with [[Necromancy]], the ability to raise the dead as undead beings, but it also covers '''Reincarnation''', characters who, upon death, eventually reincarnate as different people.

In many cases, self-resurrection may take too long to be combat applicable, but it still exists. The ability has similar limitations to regeneration, and one should not assume that, because a character has never failed to resurrect, that they can resurrect regardless of how they've been killed.

==Users==
* [[Shenron (Dragon Ball)|Shenron]] ([[Dragon Ball]])
* [[Po (Kung Fu Panda)|Po]] ([[Kung Fu Panda]])
* [[Kratos]] ([[God of War]])
* [[Doctor Doom]] ([[Marvel Comics]])
* [[Chosen Undead]] ([[Dark Souls]])
* [[Cosmo (The Fairly OddParents!)|Cosmo]] ([[The Fairly OddParents!]])
